title: Archetype
description: Unified Archetype schema and GSM meta-schema. As the seed Archetype's statement, this schema defines the structure and semantics of ALL Archetype Ascription statements (including itself — autopoietic closure). As a meta-schema, it extends JSON Schema 2020-12 to validate $gsm:* vocabulary keywords at every schema node. DM validates incoming Archetype schemas against this meta-schema at authoring time (first-pass structural gate); semantic checks (allOf chain resolution, cross-Definition uniqueness, type-compatibility, mutual exclusion) are DM application-level. The Archetype's identity is carried by title (DM-enforced identity-bound rule: MUST be non-empty, globally unique among in-effect Archetypes, and MUST NOT change across Ascriptions of the same Definition). title is used as the root identifier in Norm CEL applicability expressions. DM assigns $id inside the schema using the convention gsm://archetypes/{title}/v{version}. Bootstrap: this schema's $schema references JSON Schema 2020-12 (the bootstrap exception, mirroring archetype_id = self at the instance level). Sealed — tenant-defined archetypes MUST NOT extend Archetype.

Sealed.description: Top-level schema keyword. Marks an Archetype schema as non-extensible. Tenant-defined Archetypes MUST NOT declare allOf referencing a sealed schema.

IdentityBound.description: Property-level. Value MUST NOT change across Ascriptions of the same Definition. The identity-bound set is immutable per Archetype Definition.

Queryable.description: Property-level. DM provisions a PostgreSQL expression index on the JSONB path (B-tree for scalars, GIN for arrays/objects). Max queryable properties per Archetype: DM-configurable cap (default: 8). Property type MUST be indexable: string, number, integer, boolean, or array of scalars.

Unique.description: Property-level. Unique among in-effect (ACTIVE/DEPRECATED) Ascriptions of the same Archetype. DM provisions a partial unique expression index.

DataProtection.description: Property-level. Phase-first protection model. Two lifecycle phases (atRest, inTransit), each declaring at most one of four uniform measures (encryption, hash, mask, suppression). No auto-cascade between phases. All annotated fields are subject to implicit access audit.

DataProtectionMeasures.description: Exactly one protection measure per lifecycle phase.

keyRetention.description: Days before automatic key destruction (crypto-shredding). Omit for on-demand only.

hash.description: One-way salted hash. DM-managed salt. Irreversible.

mask.description: Partial masking. `from` selects the visible end; `with` specifies the replacement.

from.description: Which end of the value remains visible. LEFT = first characters visible (mask from the right). RIGHT = last characters visible (mask from the left).

occurrence.description: Number of characters to keep visible.

suppression.description: Complete omission — field absent from output.
